title: Listening - Part 4: AI in the Workplace
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about how artificial intelligence has affected their jobs. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’m a graphic designer, and [21]I initially started using AI tools because I was under immense pressure to increase my daily output without any extra staff[/21]. I had to find a shortcut. It’s been a learning curve. What I’ve found, though, is [26]it’s actually freed me up to focus on the more conceptual side of my work[/26]. I no longer spend hours on repetitive retouching tasks, which means I can spend more time brainstorming original ideas. It’s been a creative liberation. || Speaker 2: As a customer service manager, [22]we implemented AI chatbots primarily to deal with the sheer volume of routine enquiries we were getting[/22]. It was an efficiency drive. Interestingly, [27]the biggest gain has been the improvement in my team's morale[/27]. They’re no longer bored by answering the same ten questions every day, so they’re much more engaged when they have to handle a complex, human issue. || Speaker 3: I’m a data analyst, and [23]I was worried that AI would eventually make my role redundant, so I decided to become an expert in it before that happened[/23]. It was a survival strategy. What’s been most rewarding is [28]the way it’s enhanced the accuracy of our long-term predictions[/28]. We can spot trends that would have been invisible to a human eye, and that’s given our company a massive competitive edge. || Speaker 4: I work in HR, and [24]we used AI to help us sift through thousands of job applications in a more objective way[/24]. We wanted to eliminate any unconscious bias in the shortlisting process. It’s been very effective. But [29]I’ve also noticed how much it’s improved our internal communication[/29]. The AI tools help us draft much clearer, more consistent updates for our staff, which has reduced misunderstandings across different departments. || Speaker 5: I’m a translator, and [25]I started using AI translation software because I wanted to take on larger, more technical projects that were previously too time-consuming[/25]. I wanted to grow my business. Surprisingly, [30]it’s actually led me to develop a much more nuanced understanding of linguistics[/30]. I have to carefully edit the AI output, and that process of comparing its literal version with a more natural human one has taught me a lot about the subtleties of language.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker decided to adopt AI tools.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 was under pressure to increase 'daily output' without extra staff.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 implemented chatbots to handle the 'volume of routine enquiries'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to protect their job security by becoming an expert.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to eliminate 'unconscious bias' in recruitment.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to take on 'larger, more technical projects'.

options.A: to ensure their long-term job security
options.D: to keep up with a rival company
options.G: to ensure fairness in certain procedures
options.H: to expand the scope of their services

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most beneficial about using AI.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 can now focus on the 'conceptual' and 'brainstorming' side.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 says the team is 'more engaged' and morale has improved.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 notes the 'accuracy' of predictions has been enhanced.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 says it has led to 'clearer, more consistent updates' for staff.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 has developed a 'more nuanced understanding' of language.

options.A: the increased reliability of their findings
options.B: the financial savings they have made
options.C: the opportunity for more professional travel
options.D: the chance to prioritize high-level thinking
options.E: the speed with which they can finish projects
options.G: the growth in their specialist knowledge
options.H: the positive impact on staff motivation
